title: TNPSC Eligibility Criteria for Group 1, 2, 2A, 4 & Other Posts
excerpt: TNPSC conducts a wide range of exams to recruit the eligible candidates for various state government posts. Eligibility criteria like age limit and educational qualification differs based on the TNPSC exam posts allocated for Group 1, 2, 2A, 4 and others. The minimum qualification for most of the posts is bachelor’s degree from a recognized university
content:
TNPSC conducts a wide range of exams to recruit the eligible candidates for various state government posts. Eligibility criteria like age limit and educational qualification differs based on the TNPSC exam posts allocated for Group 1, 2, 2A, 4 and others. The minimum qualification for most of the posts is bachelor’s degree from a recognized university, while for Group 4, it is SSLC. However, the reserved category has age relaxation for the upper limits. Candidates must know about the eligibility criteria for various TNPSC posts before beginning the preparation and application process. In this article, we have provided information about TNPSC eligibility criteria in detail, so candidates can know where they fit in.

Overview of the TNPSC Exam

As mentioned, Tamil Nadu Public Service Commission (TNPSC) conducts competitive exams to recruit qualified candidates for government positions in Tamil Nadu. It is categorized into different groups – Group 1, Group 2, Group 2A, Group 4, and specialized services like Combined Engineering Services, District Educational Officer, Assistant Conservator of Forests, and Administrative Service Officer positions. The commission ensures the candidates meet some eligibility criteria before they appear on the TNPSC exam.  Generally, candidates who plan to apply for the TNPSC exam must be a citizen of India, well-versed in Tamil language, and attained required qualifications from recognized universities.

TNPSC Group 1 Exam Eligibility Criteria

For TNPSC Group 1 exam posts, candidates’ age limit typically needs to be around 21-32. Reserved categories like SC/ST/OBC have an age relaxation of up to 37 years. When coming to educational qualification, a bachelor’s degree from a recognized university under the UGC act, Central act or state act is mandatory for all Group 1 Posts like deputy collector, deputy superintendent of police and assistant commissioner. The physical requirements for posts like Deputy Superintendent of Police and District Officer needs to be met, where the height and weight measurements vary for man and woman. 

Check out: TNPSC Group 1 Exam Syllabus

TNPSC Group 2 & 2A Exam Eligibility Criteria

As for the age limit, those who apply for TNPSC group 2 and 2A posts must be aged between 18-32 in general. However, age qualifications vary for the specific posts, for instance, candidates who prefer junior employment officers must be in the age of 18-40. Reserved categories have some age relaxation for most posts. A bachelor’s degree from a recognized university is necessary for all the posts, though the specific educational qualification differs. Adequate knowledge of Tamil language is required to be eligible for writing the exam.

Also read: TNPSC Group 2 and 2A Exam Syllabus

TNPSC Group 4 Exam Eligibility Criteria

Candidates who are focusing on the TNPSC group 4 posts, must attain the minimum of age 18. For general candidates, the maximum age limit will be 30 years, while the candidates coming under the Most Backward Classes, Denotified Communities, Backward Classes, and Backward classes will be 32 years. Candidates who belonged to ST, SC, SC (A) communities and Destitute Widows of all castes will be 35 years of age. Aspirants who are applying for TNPSC Group 4 exam must pass the SSLC or equivalent qualification from a recognized board and should be qualified to enroll in higher secondary or college studies. Specializations differ based on the post one opt for in the TNPSC exam.

Find more information: TNPSC Group 4 Exam Syllabus

TNPSC Combined Engineering Services Exam Eligibility Criteria

The age limit for the candidates who are appearing for the TNPSC combined engineering services exam is between 18-30 years of age. For reserved categories, there is no upper age limit, while the lower-bar remains 18. Though the educational qualifications differ based on the posts, candidates must have a B.E or relevant degree from the university recognized under the UGC, Central government, or state government. Specializations can differ based on the posts one opting for. 

TNPSC Assistant Conservator of Forests (ACF) Exam Eligibility Criteria

Candidates who are applying for the TNPSC ACF exam must be aged between 21-32 years. For the reserved categories, the age limit changes as per the TNPSC guidelines. When it comes to educational qualifications, a bachelor’s degree in forestry, environmental science, botany, zoology, or related subjects are required. Some might require specific degrees in Forest engineering or Master’s degree in relevant fields. Candidates must meet the specific physical requirements and be able to work in forest under the challenging conditions. 

TNPSC District Educational Officer (DEO) Exam Eligibility Criteria

Typically, candidates who are appearing for the TNPSC DEO exam, fall between 32-40 years of age. There is no upper age limit for the candidates belonging to the reserved categories like ST, SC, SC (A). And, the applicant of TNPSC DEO posts must have a Master’s degree, a B.T., or a B.Ed. Degree from the recognized university. 

TNPSC ASO Exam Eligibility Criteria

For those candidates appearing for the TNPSC ASO exam, their age must fall between the 18-30 years of age. As like others, applicants from reserved categories have an age relaxation as per Tamilnadu government norms. A Bachelor’s degree from a recognized university acts as a minimum requirement for the candidates who are appearing for this exam as well. 

Summary Table of TNPSC Exam Eligibility Criteria

Below, we have given the table summarizing the TNPSC exam eligibility criteria for Group 1, Group 2 and 2A, Group 4, and other posts:

Summary Table of TNPSC Exam Eligibility Criteria

Below, we have given the table summarizing the TNPSC exam eligibility criteria for Group 1, Group 2 and 2A, Group 4, and other posts:

Note: TNPSC eligibility criteria for all groups are subject to change as per the Tamilnadu government guidelines. So, ensure to keep updated with the recent information and updates. 

What’s Next?

If you are planning to apply for the TNPSC exam and find yourself eligible, you must start preparing for the examination. Make sure to understand the syllabus of the chosen TNPSC group before getting into the preparation. Schedule the sections, study consistently and ensure timely revision to strengthen your knowledge and answering skills. To make your TNPSC preparation more easier, connect with KingMakers IAS academy, recognized as the best TNPSC coaching centre in Chennai and let our experienced mentors guide you. You will get adequate mock tests, high-quality preparation materials and personalized mentoring to achieve a high enough score to secure a government job position.

faqs.question: What is the minimum education qualification typically required for TNPSC exams?
faqs.answer: Typically, the educational qualification required for most TNPSC posts is a bachelor’s degree from a recognized university. For Group 4 posts, candidates must at least completed an SSLC or an equivalent qualification.

faqs.question: What is the age limit for the TNPSC Group 1 exam?
faqs.answer: The age limit for general candidates is usually between 21 – 34 years, while some posts like Deputy collector have an upper age limit of 32. Reserved categories such as SC/ST/OBC have an age relaxation for up to 39 years.

faqs.question: s there any age relaxation for reserved categories in TNPSC exams?
faqs.answer: Yes, candidates belonging to the reserved categories like SC, ST, MBC, BC, and Destitute Widows have upper limit age relaxations. For some exams, they have no upper age limit at all.

faqs.question: Do TNPSC exams have physical requirements?
faqs.answer: Yes, certain TNPSC posts like Deputy Superintendent of Police (DSP), District Officer, and Assistant Conservator of Forests (ACF) must meet the certain physical standards related to height, weight and overall fitness.

faqs.question: Can candidates from outside Tamil Nadu apply for TNPSC exams?
faqs.answer: Yes, candidates from outside Tamil Nadu, who are Indian citizens, can apply for TNPSC examinations, but they must demonstrate good proficiency in Tamil language.

focusKeyword: TNPSC Eligibility Criteria for Group 1, 2, 2A, 4 & Other Posts

title: How to Prepare for TNPSC Group 4 Exam ?
excerpt: TNPSC Group 4 exam syllabus comprises three main sections: the Tamil Eligibility-cum-Scoring Test (or General English, if selected), General Studies,
content:
TNPSC Group 4 exam syllabus comprises three main sections: the Tamil Eligibility-cum-Scoring Test (or General English, if selected), General Studies, and the Aptitude & Mental Ability Test. This government examination is conducted by The Tamil Nadu Public Service Commission. It allows the candidates to get positions including Village Administrative Officers (VAO), Bill Collector, Field Surveyor, Junior Assistant, Draftsman and more. To excel in the TNPSC Group 4 exam, one must have a clear preparation plan with previous year papers, mock tests, study material and revision plan. Our KingMakers IAS Academy packs all these to provide the best TNPSC Group 4 preparation for all the aspirants. 

Since the question of how to prepare for TNPSC Group 4 is frequently asked, we are committed to resolving this. In this article, you will understand the TNPSC group 4 syllabus and how to prepare for it in an effective manner.

TNPSC Group 4 – Overview and Exam Pattern

In this section, we are going to explore the TNPSC Group 4 exam overview, syllabus, exam pattern and marking scheme. 

TNPSC Group 4 Overview

TNPSC Group 4 Exam Pattern

Learn more: Everything About TNPSC Group 4 2025 Competitive Exams | Eligibility, Salary, Benefits

7 Steps to Prepare for TNPSC Group 4 Exam Well

Below, we have provided the step-by-step process on how to prepare for the TNPSC Group 4 examination. This preparation strategy provides you with the best direction to excel in the TNPSC group 4 exam.

Step 1: Create a Well-Structured Study Plan for TNPSC Group 4

Firstly, it is sensible to lay out the foundation before getting into the actual stuff. Start developing a realistic, clear and detailed study plan with daily and weekly goals. It includes a distribution of study materials to learn, mock tests to practice and question papers to review with right intervals. Ensure to include the revision plan to review the materials you’ve learned. It helps you to build long-term retention and better learning. You must be determined to achieve the goal and monitor it accordingly. 

Step 2: Examine the TNPSC Group 4 Syllabus and Exam Pattern

Before getting into the studies, make sure to get a good grasp of TNPSC group 4 syllabus and exam pattern. It gives you a clear idea on how to move around and achieve what is meant to be achieved. Typically, the TNPSC Group 4 exam has a single paper with two language options, General Tamil and General English. Further, it has three sections – General Studies with 75 questions, Aptitude and Mental Ability Test with 25 questions, and General English/Tamil with 100 questions. Overall marks are 300 with no negative marking. Aspirants can choose the language on their convenience and prepare the section-wise distribution questions accordingly.  

Step 3: Understand the Core Concepts and Topics

TNPSC aspirants must have a better conceptual clarity on fundamental concepts and topical coverage. Have a better grasp on concepts like General science, history, culture, polity, geography, and economics. And, cover subtopics comes under the three sections of TNPSC Group 4 syllabus. This work is necessary to move forward and build the knowledge, enough to crack the TNPSC Group 4 examination. 

Step 4: Build Short Notes

Build a repository of short notes and review them regularly to effectively prepare for the TNPSC Group 4 exam. When studying, candidates can jot down the important points, concepts, formulas, details and tricks in a notebook for a future reference. Use mindmap, flowcharts, diagrams, tables, and lists to store the information in a memorable way. Explain the concepts as though you are teaching the students, and help you to develop accurate conceptual clarity. Categorizing and documenting these notes would be the better revision strategy before your TNPSC Group 4 exam.

Step 5: Review Previous Year TNPSC Group 4 Question Papers

By reviewing previous year question papers, TNPSC candidates can understand the exam’s difficulty level and identify the frequently asked questions. Solving them under a stipulated time allows you to develop time management skills during examinations. The more question papers you solve, the more familiarity you can create with the TNPSC Group 4 questions. This sense of familiarity boosts your confidence to face the questions. Also, this acts as a good memory technique since you actively recall the information every time solving the question paper. 

Step 6: Practice Mock Tests and Test Series

In KingMakers IAS Academy, aspirants can find a huge amount of mock tests and test series to practice. By practicing these tests on a daily and weekly basis, candidates can achieve a better understanding of the materials. Our Good morning test helps you to keep informed on the current affairs and getting your hands on answer writing practice. Aspirants must engage in the mock tests and test series consistently to get better at the TNPSC group 4 examination. Testing yourself is always the best learning strategy since it identifies the holes and errors in your understanding and pushes you to seal it. 

Step 7: Do Consistent Revision

Regular revision is essential to remember information and attain long-term retention. Aspirants must allot a dedicated time on a weekly basis to review previously learned materials. Make sure to revise the notes you’ve built to cement the concepts and details in an effortless manner. Also, revise the solved question papers to get the sense of your mistakes and ways to improve. In his research, American Psychologist Dunlosky found interleaved practice and practice testing are the two top learning strategies. Thus, practicing the question papers and revising the learned materials at regular intervals can help you to study well and crack the TNPSC Group 4 exam. 

Preparation Tips for TNPSC Group 4

Here are several preparation tips that helps you to follow along the strategy and boost up the learning:

  • Read newspapers, magazines, articles, current affairs, editorials and GK books to build your knowledge.
  • Practice writing the essays to improve your language skills.
  • Get familiar with quantitative aptitude and logical reasoning with our mock tests.
  • Try to attempt mock exams in real-time exam conditions to improve time management skills.
  • Test yourself and identify the weak spots in your understanding.
  • Have a healthy diet, good sleep and stay motivated.
  • Don’t get into new topics or books in the last days of your examination. 

Download the complete how to prepare for TNPSC group 4 PDF article for your reference!

Conclusion

Cracking TNPSC Group 4 examination has become easier with proper preparation. We’ve developed a strong preparation framework in this blog, which you can plainly follow or modify based on your preferences. Building a preparation strategy is easy, but sticking to the plan is difficult and necessary. So, after creating a preparation plan, get committed to it and achieve daily and weekly goals. Our KingMakers IAS Academy removes this effort and helps you to achieve the best level of preparation for your TNPSC examination.

  • TNPSC Group 1 Mains Test Series
  • TNPSC 2/2A Prelims Test Series
  • TNPSC Groups 2 Mains Test Batch

faqs.question: What is the TNPSC Group 4 Examination?
faqs.answer: The TNPSC Group 4 Exam is a competitive exam, which is conducted by the Tamil Nadu Public Service Commission. This allows the government to recruit candidates for positions like Village Administrative Officer (VAO), Junior Assistant, Bill Collector, Field Surveyor, and Draftsman.

faqs.question: What is the TNPSC Group 4 Exam Pattern and its marking scheme?
faqs.answer:
TNPSC Group 4 exam is a single paper examination comprising 200 objective questions with a total of 300 marks. Its duration is about 3 hours and no negative marking. It includes:

– General Studies: 75 Questions (112.5 marks)
– Aptitude and Mental Ability: 25 Questions (37.5 marks)
– General Tamil/General English: 100 Questions (150 marks)

faqs.question: How can I prepare for the TNPSC Group 4 Exam?
faqs.answer: You can prepare for the TNPSC Group 4 Exam in the following manner – create realistic and structured study plan, divide and allocate time for each section of the syllabus, solve previous year question papers and do consistent revision.

faqs.question: What are the variety of topics covered in the TNPSC Group 4 exam?
faqs.answer:
Here are the list of topics you will face in the TNPSC Group 4 examination:
– General Studies: History, Polity, Geography, Economy, Science, and Current Affairs
– Aptitude & Mental Ability: Simplification, Time & Distance, Data Interpretation, etc.
– General Tamil / English: Grammar, Comprehension, Vocabulary, and Usage

faqs.question: How significant is to practice previous year question papers and mock tests for TNPSC Group 4 preparation?
faqs.answer: By solving the previous TNPSC group 4 exam question papers, you can understand the level of difficulty and question trends. This helps you to improve your time management skills. And, consistent engagement in mock tests allows the students to identify weak spots and boost confidence.

faqs.question: How frequently can the TNPSC Group 4 exam syllabus be revised?
faqs.answer: Consistent revision is an important element in TNPSC Group 4 preparation. Try to dedicate one day per week to review the short notes, important concepts, and solved papers. Implement spaced repetition and active recall technique to develop long-term memory retention.

title: UPSC CSAT Syllabus 2026: A Complete Overview for IAS Aspirants
excerpt: UPSC CSAT (Civil Services Aptitude Test), also known as the General Studies Paper – 2, is the second paper of the Preliminary examination. It is a qualifying paper, where the candidates have to achieve a minimum score of 33% for your General Studies Paper – 1 (GS-1) to be evaluated. It has the objective-type questions to test the UPSC candidates’
content:
UPSC CSAT (Civil Services Aptitude Test), also known as the General Studies Paper – 2, is the second paper of the Preliminary examination. It is a qualifying paper, where the candidates have to achieve a minimum score of 33% for your General Studies Paper – 1 (GS-1) to be evaluated. It has the objective-type questions to test the UPSC candidates’ comprehension, analytical ability, logical reasoning, and mental agility. To prepare for UPSC CSAT examination, IAS aspirants need dedication, strategy, commitment and consistent efforts on practicing the CSAT questions. In this blog, we are going to provide the detailed information on UPSC CSAT syllabus, allowing you to prepare well and excel in the examination. 

Do you want to make the UPSC CSAT preparation easier? Get into KingMakers IAS academy to experience the expert assistance, numerous practice tests, personalized attention and guidance & more!

Quick Facts: UPSC CSAT Syllabus 2026

  • Exam Name: UPSC Civil Services Preliminary Examination – General Studies Paper-II (CSAT)
  • Mode: Pen and Paper
  • Total Marks: 200
  • Number of Questions: 80 (Multiple Choice Questions)
  • Mark per question: 2.5 marks
  • Duration: 2 hours
  • Nature: Qualifying (You need 33% or 66.67 marks to pass).
  • Negative Marking: Yes (1/3rd of the marks allotted to the question, which is -0.83).

UPSC CSAT Syllabus – A Brief Overview

Introduced in 2011, CSAT became an essential part of the UPSC Preliminary examination. Clearing this exam is mandatory for the evaluation of GS-1 Paper, eventually influencing the qualification for UPSC Mains exam. As we mentioned, it tests the UPSC candidates’ comprehension, logical reasoning, general mathematical ability, data interpretation and basic numeracy skills to check the suitability for the Civil services.

As for the CSAT exam pattern, it has 80 questions, totalling around 200 marks, spanning for 2 hours. It is a paper and pen mode of examination, where the IAS aspirants are required to answer the multiple choice questions related to intellectual aptitudes, in general. Candidates must score the minimum of 33% (66 out of 200 marks) to be selected for GS-1 paper evaluation. Hone your strategy and keep committed to it in order to excel in the examination. 

What Aspects of the Candidates Tested in UPSC CSAT Exam?

There are a variety of aspects of the candidates that will be tested in the UPSC CSAT examination. All the skills related to the certain administrative application. In the following table, we have provided the skills that are gonna be tested and the application it entails:

Detailed UPSC CSAT Syllabus 2026 Breakdown (Topic-wise)

In this part, we are going to provide the breakdown of USPC CSAT Syllabus 2026 for a better understanding. Each section below includes 6-10 specific question types and themes commonly tested, based on UPSC CSAT syllabus and past trends.

  1. UPSC CSAT Syllabus – Comprehension
  2. UPSC CSAT Syllabus – Interpersonal skills
  3. UPSC CSAT Syllabus – Logical Reasoning
  4. UPSC CSAT Syllabus – Decision Making & Problem Solving
  5. UPSC CSAT Syllabus – General Mental Ability
  6. UPSC CSAT Syllabus – Basic Numeracy
  7. UPSC CSAT Syllabus – Data Interpretation

1. UPSC CSAT Syllabus – Comprehension

  • Main idea or central theme identification from passages
  • Author’s tone, attitude, or purpose analysis
  • Direct factual recall from the text
  • Inference-based questions on implied meanings
  • Summary or paraphrase selection for the passage
  • Title or heading choice that best fits content
  • Assumption or presupposition detection
  • Strengthening/weakening passage arguments
  • Counterargument or conclusion evaluation

2. UPSC CSAT Syllabus – Interpersonal skills

  • Appropriate response selection in conversations
  • Conflict resolution between colleagues or teams
  • Handling sensitive feedback or criticism
  • Ethical dilemmas in workplace interactions
  • Negotiation tactics in group discussions
  • Empathy demonstration in client dealings
  • Leadership communication in crisis
  • Cultural sensitivity in diverse teams

3. UPSC CSAT Syllabus – Logical Reasoning

  • Syllogism-based conclusions and contradictions
  • Assumptions underlying given statements
  • Argument strengthening or weakening options
  • Coding-decoding of words, numbers, or symbols
  • Alphabetical or numerical series completion
  • Direction sense and distance calculations
  • Seating arrangement (linear/circular)
  • Input-output machine patterns
  • Classification and odd-one-out identification

4. UPSC CSAT Syllabus – Decision Making & Problem Solving

  • Ethical choice in resource allocation dilemmas
  • Prioritization during emergencies or crises
  • Corruption or integrity testing situations
  • Team management under constraints
  • Public grievance resolution strategies
  • Risk assessment in implementation plans
  • Fairness in disciplinary actions
  • Innovation in problem-solving approaches
  • Long-term vs short-term decision impacts

5. UPSC CSAT Syllabus – General Mental Ability

  • Number, letter, or figure series completion
  • Direction and displacement calculations
  • Venn diagram-based set relationships
  • Mirror/water image reflections
  • Embedded figures or figure completion
  • Cube/dice unfolding and counting
  • Analogy questions (verbal/non-verbal)

6. UPSC CSAT Syllabus – Basic Numeracy

  • Simplification and BODMAS operations
  • HCF/LCM of numbers and applications
  • Fractions, decimals, and percentage conversions
  • Profit/loss, discount, and marked price
  • Simple/compound interest calculations
  • Ratio, proportion, and variation problems
  • Time-speed-distance and relative speed
  • Time-work efficiency and pipes-cisterns
  • Mensuration (area, volume, perimeter)

7. UPSC CSAT Syllabus – Data Interpretation

  • Bar graphs for category comparisons
  • Pie charts for percentage distributions
  • Line graphs tracking changes over time
  • Table data extraction and calculations
  • Data sufficiency yes/no judgments
  • Ratio or proportion from datasets
  • Multi-graph comparison questions

Make use of the CSAT classes conducted by our KingMakers Faculty Mr. Ayyapan to strengthen your answering skills required to clear the UPSC CSAT examination,

Preparation Tips to Crack the Questions Comes Under UPSC CSAT Examination

Below, we have given the best strategies and tips for your UPSC CSAT (Civil Services Aptitude Test) examination:

  • Understand the Syllabus: It will always be a first and foremost task, regardless of the examination. Ensure to understand the UPSC CSAT syllabus well in order to boost your preparation. 
  • Practice Reading Daily: Focus on allocating the reading tasks daily and check it off. This daily practice elevates the comprehension skills of the UPSC candidates to new heights.
  • Gain Mastery in Basic Numeracy: Even though maths might not be the strongest areas of yours, acquiring the Class 10 mathematical skills like percentages, simple interest, time and work can lead you a long way. 
  • Develop Reasoning Skills: Only through practice, you can improve your reasoning skills. So, practice the riddles, solve puzzles, and do online mock tests to sharpen your reasoning capacity.
  • Solve Previous year question papers: This enables you to get the sense of question trends and difficulty levels of the question types. Analyzing and practicing the previous year question papers can greatly increase the possibility of cracking the UPSC CSAT exam.

Below, we have provided the complete playlist containing the tips and strategies to solve the questions in the UPSC CSAT examination,
